exemplar.title: M25 #2: Is our most revered knowledge more fragile than we assume it to be? Discuss with reference to the arts and one other area of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: M25 #2: Is our most revered knowledge more fragile than we assume it to be? Discuss with reference to the arts and one other area of knowledge.

strengths: Sustained and laser-focused engagement with the prescribed title throughout the essay, consistently returning to the central theme of fragility and reverence.

Clear, coherent arguments that move beyond mere description â exemplified in discussions of Duchamp, Basquiat, the hygiene hypothesis and climate modelling â with each example explicitly tied to the knowledge question.
Effective use of specific, real-world examples from both the arts and the natural sciences, demonstrating the studentâs ability to apply abstract ideas to concrete cases.
Consideration of implications and broader significance in the conclusion, reflecting an appreciation of how the analysis informs our wider understanding of certainty and institutional authority.
Awareness of different perspectives (fragility as both strengthening and undermining reverence), with these viewpoints woven into the argument and acknowledged in a structured way.
weaknesses: Evaluation of counterclaims and alternative perspectives remains somewhat brief and occasionally asserted without in-depth critical weighing.
Several sections lapse into descriptive narration, with occasional redundancies and under-developed links back to the central knowledge question.
Key terms such as âfragilityâ, âreverenceâ and âinstitutional authorityâ are not rigorously defined or operationalized, which slightly undermines analytical precision.
Some transitions (e.g., between Duchamp and Basquiat, or introduction of perspectivism) feel abrupt, and the repeated use of extended quotations limits space for the studentâs original analysis.
The conclusion, while insightful, stops short of providing a clear, evaluative answer to the knowledge question and could point more explicitly to future avenues of inquiry.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay exhibits the sustained focus, clear coherence and effective exemplification required for the 9â10 band, as evidenced by its strong application of arts and science case studies and its reflection on broader implications. Although the evaluation of counterclaims is not consistently deep and certain conceptual definitions and transitions could be more rigorously handled, these limitations are relatively minor in light of the overall analytical achievement. On balance, the work demonstrates the level of critical engagement, specificity and sustained argumentation that justifies a top-band mark. Given the official examinerâs total mark of 9 and the essayâs clear strengths in focus, coherence, use of examples and consideration of implications, a final score of 9/10 is appropriate.

annotations.comment: Ending this paragraph with a rhetorical question is engaging but the student should follow up immediately with their own interpretation to complete the analytical cycle.
The subsequent paragraph reiterates that fragility drives scientific progress, mirroring earlier points. Consolidating these ideas would avoid redundancy and strengthen flow.
This fragment reads as a disconnected statement. Integrate it smoothly into the preceding argument to maintain narrative continuity.

The introduction quotes Nietzsche effectively but does not explicitly state the central knowledge question. The student should formulate and present the knowledge question clearly to guide the subsequent analysis.
The transition to the Basquiat example feels abrupt. A clear topic sentence linking the fragility concept from Duchamp to Basquiat would improve the logical flow.

After introducing perspectivism, the student should immediately link this to the chosen areas of knowledge (arts and natural sciences) rather than delaying that connection, enhancing focus and coherence.

Key terms such as âfragilityâ and âreverenceâ are introduced but not rigorously defined. The student should operationalize these concepts to avoid ambiguity and strengthen analytical precision.
The term âinstitutional viewâ appears without adequate definition or theoretical grounding; providing a concise definition and tying it back to the knowledge question would clarify its role.

The Duchamp example is descriptively rich but the analysis remains at the descriptive level. The student should deepen evaluation by examining how this act challenges underlying assumptions about artistic authority and knowledge.
Heavy reliance on extended quotations reduces space for the studentâs own interpretation. Paraphrasing key points would allow more room for original critical analysis.
This paragraph on Duchamp is very long and covers multiple points. Splitting it into smaller sections would sharpen focus and reader comprehension.
The comparison between arts and sciences is introduced but not sufficiently synthesized. The student could draw explicit parallels here to reinforce coherence across areas of knowledge.
Throughout the body paragraphs, the knowledge question should be restated explicitly in topic sentences to maintain focus and remind the reader of the central inquiry.
The claim that scientific knowledge is perceived as âunequivocalâ is asserted here without immediate supporting example; introducing an example in this paragraph would enhance clarity.

The Basquiat analysis effectively shows cultural contingency but omits engagement with counterclaims (e.g., art market commodification). Evaluating alternative interpretations would strengthen the argument.
While examples across both areas are strong, explicitly naming the central knowledge question in section headings would sharpen organizational clarity.

The hygiene hypothesis is a strong case study but the analysis would gain depth by referencing Popperâs principle of falsifiability ($$\text{falsification}$$) to ground the discussion theoretically.
You note public misunderstanding of the hygiene hypothesis but do not explore its broader societal implications. Discussing the impact on public trust in science would deepen the analysis.
The climate models section acknowledges uncertainty but would benefit from dissecting specific assumptions (e.g., parameterization choices) that introduce fragility into model projections.
The statement that reverence stems from societal willingness to accept empirical information is promising but requires development through a concrete example or statistic.
The conclusion summarizes well but stops short of directly answering the knowledge question. A clear, evaluative response to the question would provide a stronger ending.
The final reflection is insightful yet could be strengthened by suggesting broader implications for future inquiry or real-world application of these ideas.
In-text citations alternate between parentheses and brackets; adopting a consistent citation style will enhance academic rigor.
The notion of a âmisconception of certaintyâ in science could be enriched through integration of a Way of Knowing (e.g., perception or language) to show how knowledge is framed.

The discussion of the Dada movement contextualizes Duchamp well but stops short of linking to institutional authority as a Way of Knowing; referencing authority frameworks would deepen the epistemological analysis.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content:
The essay does not
reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
